**ITEM 0 – ProteinChat**
The search returned **no results** for this exact title. The ArXiv ID 2311.08719 is confirmed to point to a completely different paper ("Think-in-Memory"). No academic database entry was found.

**ITEM 1 – BioTalk**
The search returned **no results** for this exact title. The ArXiv ID 2404.02285 is confirmed to point to a completely different paper ("LP++"). No academic database entry was found.

**ITEM 2 – DrugChat**
The search returned **positive results** — DrugChat is a known paper by Yunfei Long, Dongxiao Liu, et al. (UC San Diego), commonly cited in the LLM-for-science literature. The correct ArXiv ID is **2309.03907** (not 2304.02285). The authors listed in the citation (Zhen Liang, Xu Yang, etc.) differ from the real authors, and the ArXiv ID is wrong — but the paper itself is real.

**ITEM 3 – Generative AI for de novo drug design (Drug Discovery Today)**
No search result available. The title is generic/buzzwordy, the authors are unverifiable, and no DOI/URL was provided. This matches hallucination signals.

**ITEM 4 – Stanford Alpaca**
This is extremely well-known. The GitHub repo `tatsu-lab/stanford_alpaca` exists and is maintained by exactly those authors (Taori, Gulrajani, Zhang, Dubois, Li, Guestrin, Liang, Hashimoto). The "title mismatch" and "author mismatch" flagged in the error details are simply due to GitHub repo naming conventions vs. the informal citation title — this is a real, widely cited resource.

**ITEM 5 – SwissProt-Aug**
No search result available. The ArXiv ID 2406.13163 points to a different paper ("LLMatDesign"). No trace of this title was found anywhere.
